data.title: Ethereum Supply Concentration Increases Among Top Addresses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Glassnode indicates that as of Wednesday, the top 1% of addresses hold 97.6% of the Ethereum supply, an increase from 96.1% a year ago.

Despite this concentration, Iliya Kalchev, a scheduling analyst at digital asset platform Nexo, notes that the market reaction remains "restrained." Stable capital inflows and rising derivatives trading activity suggest that investors are selectively rebuilding portfolios rather than actively rotating across the financial system.

data.title: 35% Bitcoin Correction And Hash Ribbon Bottom Signal Put SUBBD Token On Trader Radar
data.description:
What to Know: Hash Ribbon recovery after a 35% Bitcoin correction, coupled with depressed hashprice, aligns with historic late‑stage capitulation and potential cyclical bottoms. Miner stress and hashrate pullbacks often precede cleaner supply dynamics, setting the stage for capital rotations into higher‑beta narratives like AI and creator tokens. AI‑powered creator platforms are targeting opaque fees, arbitrary bans, and fragmented tooling as Web2 subscription and fan platforms continue to extract oversized revenue shares. SUBBD combines Web3 payments with AI assistants, voice cloning, and token‑gated content to help creators keep more earnings and automate fan engagement
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin-hash-ribbon-bottom-subbd-token-creator-economy/

data.title: Arthur Hayes Predicts Shift in U.S. Stock Pricing by 2026
data.description: According to Odaily, Arthur Hayes has predicted that by the end of 2026, the pricing of the largest U.S. stocks will transition to blockchain platforms. He suggests that the market focus will shift from the Nasdaq to perpetual contract charts, facilitated by 24/7, global, and intermediary-free trading.

data.title: Ethereum's Wealthiest Investors Continue Accumulation Amid Market Downturn
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, while some early Ethereum investors are cashing out, the wealthiest Ether holders are quietly accumulating the cryptocurrency despite the market's challenges. An Ethereum initial coin offering (ICO) participant recently sold $60 million worth of Ether, achieving a remarkable 9,500-fold return on investment over 11 years. This investor initially purchased Ether at approximately $0.31 per token, spending $79,000 on 254,000 tokens, which are now valued at over $757 million, as reported by blockchain data platform Lookonchain.

Following this sale, the ICO participant's wallet, identified as '0x2Eb,' retained $9.3 million in Ether, according to crypto intelligence platform Nansen. While some in the crypto community commend the investor's patience, others express concern that such profit-taking might signal potential market declines. However, the activity of this investor does not suggest panic selling but rather a consistent strategy of profit-taking, as the wallet has been gradually reducing its holdings since early September.

Despite these developments, the top 1% of Ether holders continue their accumulation undeterred by the market downturn. The proportion of Ether held by these addresses increased to 97.6% on Wednesday, up from 96.1% a year ago, according to blockchain data platform Glassnode. Additionally, U.S. spot Ether exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have resumed their accumulation, recovering after eight consecutive days of net outflows. These ETFs recorded $60 million in net positive inflows on Wednesday, marking the fourth consecutive day of gains, as noted by Farside Investors.

Despite the constructive inflows into Ether ETFs and the upcoming Ethereum Fusaka upgrade, the market's response remains measured, according to Iliya Kalchev, a dispatch analyst at digital asset platform Nexo. Kalchev explained that the combination of steady inflows and increased derivatives activity indicates that investors are selectively rebuilding exposure rather than aggressively rotating across the market. This cautious approach reflects a strategic rebuilding of positions amid ongoing market fluctuations.

data.title: Crypto bull market signal: ERC-20 stablecoin supply preserves $185B record
data.description:

source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-bull-market-signal-ethereum-erc-20-stablecoin-supply-185b-record?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Opinion: Key bull market signal – ERC-20 stablecoin supply remains at a record $185 billion.
data.description: According to the latest research from on-chain analytics platform CryptoQuant, despite the recent crypto market correction, the supply of ERC-20 stablecoins remains at an all-time high of $185 billion, considered a classic bullish signal for continued growth in the crypto market. CryptoQuant contributor XWIN Research Japan stated that the total supply of ERC-20 stablecoins on the Ethereum network alone has reached an all-time high of $185 billion by 2025 and continues to hover around that level this month. The research argues that the growth in stablecoin supply is more consistent with Bitcoin price, directly reflecting the capital entering the crypto ecosystem. Simultaneously, the research emphasizes that stablecoin supply is a better benchmark for measuring industry performance than the global M2 money supply. Furthermore, CryptoQuant points out that stablecoin reserves on Binance, the largest cryptocurrency exchange, have "surged," contrasting sharply with declining reserves of Bitcoin and ETH. This suggests that traders may have taken profits at high prices and are holding large amounts of "dry powder," waiting for a price correction or macroeconomic stabilization as fuel for a new round of explosive growth.

data.title: The AB Foundation donated HK$10 million to the Hong Kong General Association for the Promotion of Peaceful Reunification of China in upholding the "Lion Rock Spirit".
data.description: On the afternoon of November 26, 2025, a level-5 fire broke out at Hung Fook Court in Tai Po, New Territories, Hong Kong, causing significant casualties and property damage. This severe disaster touched the hearts of the entire Hong Kong and global Chinese communities, prompting urgent action from all sectors to support affected residents through donations, supplies, and volunteer services. Against this backdrop, the AB Charitable Foundation donated HK$10 million to the Hong Kong General Association for the Promotion of Peaceful Reunification of China to jointly participate in the fire relief and post-disaster community reconstruction work at Hung Fook Court, Tai Po, and to jointly promote the sustainable development of humanitarian aid and related public undertakings. The AB Charitable Foundation was established in Ireland in May 2025, dedicated to promoting global public welfare, with a particular focus on vulnerable groups affected by war and natural disasters, especially women and children. This donation not only reflects the AB Charitable Foundation's core mission of "Tech for Good" but also emphasizes a holistic approach, transcending regional, cultural, and ethnic boundaries to build a truly united and mutually supportive community with a shared future for mankind. About the AB Charitable Foundation: The AB Charitable Foundation is an independent international non-governmental organization registered in Ireland and enjoying EU legal status. Its technical and financial support comes from AB DAO. The foundation is committed to using cutting-edge technologies such as blockchain and artificial intelligence to build a transparent, trustworthy, and traceable public infrastructure, thereby promoting the sustainable development of public services such as education, healthcare, environment, and humanitarian assistance.

data.title: CME Globex Futures and Options Market Pre-Opening Schedule Announced
data.description:
According to PANews, the CME Globex futures and options market is set to begin pre-opening at 7:00 a.m. Central Time (UTC+8 21:00) and will officially open at 7:30 a.m. Central Time (UTC+8 21:30). 

The CME Globex electronic platform offers thousands of futures options with various expiration dates, along with inter-product spread contracts. The platform covers a wide range of product categories, including metals such as gold and silver, energy like crude oil, interest rates, agricultural products, stock indices, foreign exchange, as well as real estate and weather futures and options. 

Currently, over 90% of futures contracts at the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) can be electronically traded and matched on the Globex platform.

data.title: Bitcoin Dominance Weakens as ETH/BTC Strengthens, Signaling a Potential Altseason Shift
data.description:
Bitcoin dominance is beginning to soften, according to the latest analysis shared by Bitcoinsensus. Their chart shows a decisive downturn in BTC’s market share, even as price action across the broader market remains mixed. Historically, weakening dominance during a late-cycle consolidation has often preceded stronger performance from Ethereum and high-liquidity altcoins
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-dominance-weakens-as-eth-btc-strengthens-signaling-a-potential-altseason-shift/

data.title: Tether suspends Bitcoin mining operations in Uruguay due to rising energy costs.
data.description: A Tether spokesperson confirmed on Friday that Tether, the world's largest issuer of the stablecoin USDT, has suspended its Bitcoin mining operations in Uruguay due to rising energy costs, but remains committed to its long-term projects in Latin America. This confirmation comes just weeks after Tether denied reports of withdrawing from Uruguay. Previously, reports indicated a $4.8 million debt dispute between Tether and Uruguay's state-owned power company, UTE. According to local news agency El Observador on Tuesday, Tether had formally notified the Uruguayan Ministry of Labor of the suspension of mining activities and the layoff of 30 employees. Tether initially announced the launch of a "sustainable Bitcoin mining operation" in Uruguay in May 2023, aiming to utilize the country's renewable energy sources. It was reported that Tether originally planned to invest $500 million in mining operations in Uruguay. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Tether Halts Bitcoin Mining in Uruguay Amid Rising Energy Costs
data.description:
According to Odaily, Tether, the issuer of the world's largest stablecoin USDT, has confirmed the suspension of its Bitcoin mining operations in Uruguay due to increasing energy costs. Despite this pause, Tether remains committed to its long-term projects in Latin America.

This confirmation comes just weeks after Tether denied reports of withdrawing from Uruguay. Earlier reports indicated a $4.8 million debt dispute between Tether and Uruguay's state-owned power company, UTE.

Local news outlet El Observador reported on Tuesday that Tether has officially informed Uruguay's Ministry of Labor about the suspension of mining activities and the dismissal of 30 employees. Tether initially announced its "sustainable Bitcoin mining business" in Uruguay in May 2023, aiming to leverage the country's renewable energy resources. The company had reportedly planned to invest $500 million in the mining venture.
